experiences.intentions: The board is completely full, so only moves that create merges are legal/valuable. Checking rows and columns: left/right produce no adjacent equal tiles in any row, so they likely do nothing. Vertical checks show column 0 has [4,16,2,2], giving a merge on the bottom pair if moving down; column 3 has [2,4,2,4], no merge; column 1 and 2 also have no equal adjacencies. Moving up would also merge the 2s in column 0, but down places the resulting 4 at the bottom and tends to keep larger tiles lower, which is slightly preferable for stability. Goal is to make space and preserve structure; down best achieves that while the horizontal moves appear ineffective.
